issues_classified.description: EDA ordering claim is factually incorrect. Section 6.2 states 'EDA ordering (late > partial > early) holds at L12-65k (KW p=0.0002)' but phase2a_taxonomy.json shows eda_ordering_holds=false for both L12-16k and L12-65k at all tested thresholds. At L12-65k: partial median EDA (0.652) < early median EDA (0.668) — the full three-way ordering is violated. Table 3 describes early absorption as having 'Low (decoder-absent)' EDA, contradicted by data. This was BLOCKING in iter_0 and remains present in the paper.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace Section 6.2 claim with: 'The ordering late > early holds at all 5 tested thresholds for both configurations (threshold-stable; L12-65k KW p=0.0002; L12-16k KW p=0.237, underpowered n=16). The predicted full ordering late > partial > early fails: partial-type latents have lower median EDA than early-type latents at both configurations and all thresholds. This suggests decoder-coverage threshold (tau) does not correspond to EDA rank ordering.' Correct Table 3's EDA column for early absorption subtype to reflect actual data (early-type has higher EDA than partial-type). Estimated effort: 1 hour writing. Verification: eda_ordering_holds field in phase2a_taxonomy.json matches the text in Section 6.2.
issues_classified.verification: Section 6.2 text matches eda_ordering_holds=false; Table 3 EDA column for early-type corrected to reflect actual median EDA > partial-type

issues_classified.description: EDA (1 - cosine(encoder_j, decoder_j)) is mathematically identical to SAEBench encoder_decoder_cosine_sim (Pearson r > 0.999; R4 delta AUROC = 0.0). Introduction presents EDA as a new metric without disclosing this prior art. The entire novelty claim rests on Theorem 1, which is a proof sketch. A reviewer familiar with SAEBench will flag this immediately.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add to Section 3.1 before the metric definition: 'The cosine similarity between encoder row j and decoder column j has been reported in SAEBench (Karvonen et al., 2025) under the label encoder_decoder_cosine_sim. EDA's contributions are (1) a formal lower bound theorem connecting encoder-decoder misalignment to absorption degree, (2) the first systematic AUROC evaluation against supervised absorption labels across multiple SAE configurations and model families, and (3) characterization of the operating regime (mid-layer, narrow SAEs). EDA does not introduce a novel formula; its contribution is theoretical grounding and empirical validation of an existing signal.' Revise Introduction accordingly to remove 'we introduce EDA' language. Estimated effort: 1 hour writing. Verification: Introduction and Section 3.1 explicitly name SAEBench encoder_decoder_cosine_sim and distinguish EDA's contributions from it.
issues_classified.verification: Section 3.1 and Introduction explicitly name SAEBench prior metric and reposition EDA as empirical validation

issues_classified.description: Theorem 1 (EDA Lower Bound) proof sketch is circular and Appendix B is missing. The critical claim — that absorption at a partial SDL minimum produces a gradient perturbation proportional to delta * ||d_c|| * sin(theta_{jc}) — is asserted, not derived from stationarity conditions. Delta-absorption has no formal mathematical definition before the theorem statement. The bound delta^2*sin^2(theta_{jc})/(2+delta^2) appears without algebraic steps. Appendix B is referenced twice (Sections 3.3 and 7.1) but absent from paper.md.
issues_classified.suggestion: Two options: (a) Write Appendix B with genuine proof: formally define delta-absorption as the perpendicular displacement magnitude at a partial SDL minimum, state first-order stationarity conditions of the biconvex SDL loss with respect to encoder weights, derive algebraically how child-active inputs create a gradient bias, and show the cosine-distance bound algebraically. (b) Downgrade Theorem 1 to 'Proposition 1 (Intuition)' and add: 'A formal proof requires specifying the biconvex SDL landscape geometry at partial minima, which we leave to future work. The bound is empirically consistent with SynthSAEBench results in Section 3.4.' Write Appendix B with the D-EDA conditioning analysis regardless. Estimated effort: 2 days for formal proof; 1 hour for downgrade + Appendix B minimal content. Verification: Theorem 1 either has step-by-step algebraic derivation in Appendix B, or is labeled Proposition 1 (Intuition) with explicit caveats; Appendix B exists in paper.md.
issues_classified.verification: Theorem 1 has formal proof in appendix or is downgraded to Proposition; Appendix B present in paper.md

issues_classified.description: The three-subtype taxonomy's 'early absorption dominance' finding (72-75%) rests on n=16 (L12-16k, KW p=0.237 — non-significant) and n=65 (L12-65k). The finding is highly threshold-sensitive (tau=0.2: early=32%; tau=0.3: 72%; tau=0.4: 95%). Expansion to all 6 Gemma Scope + 2 GPT-2 configurations was HIGH priority in iter_0 but was not executed. GPT-2 L6 has n_pos=67 exact labels — ideal for taxonomy analysis as a third configuration.
issues_classified.suggestion: Run taxonomy analysis (early/late/partial classification at tau=0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4) on: (a) all 6 Gemma Scope configurations using the same Neuronpedia proxy labels as Phase 1; (b) GPT-2 L6 using exact Chanin et al. labels (n_pos=67, already available from Phase 5). Report early/late/partial prevalence across all configurations at multiple thresholds. If dominance holds at 70%+ across 4+ configurations, the finding is defensible. If not, downgrade from 'early dominance' to 'plurality at tau=0.3 in two configurations.' Add a threshold sensitivity table in main text. Estimated effort: 2-3 GPU hours. Verification: Taxonomy results in experiment output JSON for 4+ SAE configurations; early proportion reported at tau=0.2, 0.3, and 0.4 with counts.
issues_classified.verification: Taxonomy analysis completed for 4+ SAE configurations; threshold sensitivity table in main text

issues_classified.description: All Gemma Scope AUROC values use Neuronpedia proxy labels. The R4 access check (r4a_gemma_access_check) confirmed Gemma 2 2B remains HuggingFace-gated. This is the single highest-leverage unresolved issue: if 3+/6 Gemma configurations pass AUROC >= 0.65 with exact labels, the score rises from 5.5 to 7.0. The L12-16k pilot result (0.853) that collapsed to 0.468 in the full run at L12-65k suggests the L12-16k proxy label result (0.776) may also be inflated.
issues_classified.suggestion: Pursue Gemma 2 2B HuggingFace access via the research access application (typically 1-3 days admin). Alternatively, identify another gated model with public SAE releases and labeled absorption data. If neither is feasible within iteration budget, make this the most prominent caveat in the paper: 'All Gemma Scope AUROC values use Neuronpedia proxy labels because Gemma 2 2B remains gated on HuggingFace at time of writing; the only exact-label validation is GPT-2 Small L6 (AUROC=0.629, n_pos=67).' Do not attempt to claim Gemma results are as reliable as GPT-2 results. Estimated effort: 1 day admin + 3-5 GPU hours for re-run. Verification: Phase 1 re-run with exact Chanin et al. labels produces AUROC for all 6 Gemma configurations.
issues_classified.verification: Gemma access obtained and Phase 1 re-run with exact labels, or paper prominently discloses proxy label limitation with quantified uncertainty

issues_classified.description: D-EDA/EDA equivalence tension is unresolved. R4 direct validation shows EDA = 1 - dec_cos as a mathematical identity (delta AUROC = 0.0 on both GPT-2 configurations). But R3 Phase 5 shows D-EDA AUROC = 0.762 vs EDA AUROC = 0.336 at GPT2-L10 — requiring D-EDA to be a genuinely different calculation. The paper does not reconcile this inconsistency. Table 1's D-EDA column and Section 3.3's LASSO decomposition description are potentially misleading.
issues_classified.suggestion: Clarify the D-EDA implementation: (a) If D-EDA is the genuine LASSO-based directional decomposition described in Section 3.3, its AUROC should differ from scalar EDA. Re-implement D-EDA using the LASSO formulation and compute AUROC on GPT-2 configurations; if the result matches R3 (AUROC=0.762 at GPT2-L10), the R4 validation was computing scalar EDA not D-EDA. (b) If D-EDA is merely negated decoder cosine, rename it and remove Section 3.3's LASSO decomposition description. Update Table 1 accordingly. The R4 finding (delta=0.0) suggests (b) is correct, but this must be verified against the actual R3 implementation code. Estimated effort: 2-4 hours code review + recomputation. Verification: R3 D-EDA computation code audited and confirmed to either match or differ from scalar EDA; Table 1 corrected accordingly.
issues_classified.verification: D-EDA implementation clarified; Table 1 and Section 3.3 consistent with actual computation

issues_classified.description: AUROC is used as primary metric throughout but is unreliable at extreme class imbalance. At L12-65k: n_pos=16/65536 = 0.024% positive prevalence makes AUROC statistically unstable. AUPRC values exist in source data (phase1_eda_deda_validation.json: AUPRC = 0.0024 at L12-16k) but are not in Table 1 or discussed in main text. This was HIGH priority in iter_0 (issue_07) and was not addressed in iter_1.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add AUPRC as co-primary metric in Table 1 for all 65k-width configurations (where positive prevalence < 0.05%). Compute AUPRC from cached Phase 1 results (data already available). Add a sentence to Section 4 discussion: 'At positive prevalences below 0.1%, AUROC becomes unstable; we report AUPRC as a complementary metric for 65k-width configurations.' Add a footnote noting that AUPRC = 0.0024 at L12-65k indicates absorption enrichment remains low despite AUROC differences. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours (AUPRC computation if not cached; writing). Verification: Table 1 includes AUPRC column; Section 4 discusses metric choice under class imbalance.
issues_classified.verification: Table 1 includes AUPRC column for 65k-width configs; Section 4 discusses AUROC instability under class imbalance

issues_classified.description: Abstract DeLong comparison is ambiguous and factually misleading. The abstract states 'outperforming the decoder cosine baseline by +0.396 AUROC' immediately after 'AUROC = 0.776 on Gemma Scope L12-16k,' making readers infer +0.396 is the L12-16k advantage. The +0.396 is from L5-16k (EDA=0.698, decoder cosine=0.302); the L12-16k difference is +0.553. This is a factual error that will be immediately flagged by reviewers.
issues_classified.suggestion: Revise abstract to: 'EDA achieves AUROC = 0.776 on Gemma Scope L12-16k [0.700, 0.863], outperforming the decoder cosine baseline by +0.553 AUROC at this configuration (DeLong p ≈ 0), and AUROC = 0.629 on GPT-2 Small L6 [0.561, 0.692] with exact Chanin et al. labels.' This correctly attributes the comparison and introduces the GPT-2 exact-label anchor. Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: Abstract DeLong comparison figure matches the L12-16k result in phase1_eda_deda_validation.json.
issues_classified.verification: Abstract DeLong comparison cites L12-16k figure (+0.553); no ambiguity between L5-16k and L12-16k

issues_classified.description: Figure 1 (core method diagram: absorption mechanism and EDA geometry) is referenced in Introduction and Section 3.1 ('Figure 1a', 'Figure 1b') but only exists as fig1_absorption_mechanism_desc.md (text descriptor). No PDF was generated. A NeurIPS submission without a method diagram for the paper's core geometric mechanism is a significant gap. The '(see end-of-paper Figures section)' parenthetical is non-standard.
issues_classified.suggestion: Generate Figure 1 as a PDF: a geometric diagram illustrating (a) the trained SAE decoder column d_j and encoder row w_{e,j} for a non-absorbed latent (aligned) and an absorbed latent (misaligned due to child absorption), and (b) the EDA geometric interpretation showing the angular deviation theta_{jc}. Use matplotlib or tikz. Place in writing/figures/fig1_absorption_mechanism.pdf. Remove '(see end-of-paper Figures section)' parenthetical and replace with standard 'Figure 1' inline reference. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours. Verification: writing/figures/fig1_absorption_mechanism.pdf exists; Introduction and Section 3.1 references are standard.
issues_classified.verification: writing/figures/fig1_absorption_mechanism.pdf exists; fig references in Introduction and Section 3.1 are standard

issues_classified.description: Section 7.1 practical recommendation claims 'focusing on the top 5% of latents by EDA contains approximately 56% of absorbed latents (Prec@50 = 0.0035), reducing supervised evaluation budget by 20x.' These figures are arithmetically inconsistent with source data: top 5% of 16,384 = 819 candidates; 819 × 0.0035 ≈ 2.87 absorbed; 2.87/16 positives ≈ 18%, not 56%. The 20x figure is unexplained. Both figures are unsourced.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace with the correctly derived claim: 'At L12-16k, Prec@50 = 0.0035 — absorbed latents are 3.5x enriched in the top-50 EDA candidates versus random selection (baseline positive rate 16/16,384 = 0.098%). Prioritizing the top 5% of the dictionary (819 latents) for supervised inspection reduces the evaluation surface by 20x while maintaining meaningful enrichment of absorbed latents in the inspection set.' Verify the 20x figure is simply 1/0.05 = 20 (inspection budget reduction by dictionary fraction), not a coverage claim. Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: Claim uses correct Prec@50 calculation without unsupported '56%' figure.
issues_classified.verification: Section 7.1 contains correctly-derived Prec@50 claim without '56%' figure

issues_classified.description: ITAC null test on early-type latents does not test what it claims. Table 4's 'NULL' row for early/partial latents reports 0.00% FN reduction — but ITAC was never applied to early-type latents. The 0.00% reflects no correction being attempted, not a selectivity test. The claim 'validates subtype selectivity' is tautological.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either: (a) Apply ITAC correction procedure to a sample of early-type latents, attempt D-EDA decomposition and parent candidate identification, and report that 0/n_early latents have identifiable parent candidates (n_parent_candidates=0 for all early-type by construction since max_cos(d_k, v_p) < tau). This is the genuine null test. (b) Remove the NULL row from Table 4 and replace with: 'ITAC not applied to early-type latents (by construction: no decoder column d_k satisfies max_cos(d_k, v_p) >= tau for early-type latents). Parent candidate detection confirms 0 applicable targets, consistent with taxonomy prediction.' Estimated effort: 1-2 hours (option a) or 30 minutes (option b). Verification: NULL row either contains actual ITAC application results or is honestly labeled 'not applied.'
issues_classified.verification: Table 4 NULL row contains actual ITAC application or is explicitly labeled 'not applied'

issues_classified.description: H6 scaling analysis uses canonical estimated L0 values (range 65-72, no variation within layer-matched width comparisons). The partial rho(width | L0) cannot separate width from L0 effects when L0 does not vary within the controlled comparison. The 'H6 falsified via partial correlation' language is methodologically invalid. This was flagged in iter_0 issue_08 and remains unchanged.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace the partial correlation claim with: 'H6 cannot be properly tested with the current 6 SAE configurations because L0 values do not vary within layer-matched width comparisons (L0 range: 65-72, identical at the same layer regardless of width). We report only the directional marginal finding: wider SAEs show weakly higher absorption rates (marginal Spearman rho=0.353, p=0.495). A proper test of width vs. L0 effects requires SAE configurations with measured L0 variation within layer-matched comparisons.' Remove 'H6 falsified' language. Estimated effort: 1 hour writing. Verification: Section 7.3 H6 discussion contains L0 variation caveat; no partial correlation claim.
issues_classified.verification: H6 section removes partial correlation claim; discloses L0 insufficient variation

issues_classified.description: Three citations missing or unverified: (a) DeLong test first used in Section 4.1 without citation; (b) SynthSAEBench citation 'synthasbench2026' / arXiv:2602.14687 unverified — if internally constructed, the external citation is invalid; (c) Appendix C (RAVEL probe details) is referenced in outline but absent from paper.md. These were in iter_0 issue_09 and remain unresolved.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add 'DeLong & DeLong (1988)' citation at first use in Section 4.1. Verify arXiv:2602.14687 exists; if not, replace synthasbench2026 with: 'a synthetic benchmark constructed following the absorption injection procedure in Appendix A.' Write Appendix C with two items: (1) RAVEL probe accuracy table for city-continent/city-country/city-language; (2) complete 9-row shuffled control results table from r4b_shuffled_control.json. Estimated effort: 1 hour. Verification: DeLong citation present at first use; SynthSAEBench citation resolved; Appendix C present in paper.md.
issues_classified.verification: DeLong citation at first use; SynthSAEBench citation verified or removed; Appendix C written

issues_classified.description: Section 5.3 intra-RAVEL rho=0.924 inconsistency: this figure comes from the full R3 experiment (n=6 SAE configs, phase3e_crossdomain_analysis.json), while the shuffled control was run on a pilot subset (n=3 configs). Reviewers who notice both sample sizes will question whether the coherence would survive the shuffled control on all 6 configs. The R4 pilot with n=3 gives rho=0.667 (lower than 0.924), suggesting the R3 rho=0.924 may be sensitive to configuration selection.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add at the opening of Section 5.3: 'The intra-RAVEL coherence reported below derives from the full R3 cross-domain experiment (n=6 SAE configurations), while the shuffled hierarchy control in Section 5.2 was run on a pilot subset of 3 configurations. The coherence rho=0.924 is therefore not directly tested by the shuffled control, which targets absolute absorption rate validity rather than rank coherence. A pilot coherence estimate on the 3-configuration subset gives rho=0.667, suggesting the full-experiment rho=0.924 may be sensitive to configuration selection. Same-model probe replication on a larger configuration set is required to establish genuine cross-domain absorption structure.' Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: Section 5.3 contains the clarifying sentence with both n=6 and n=3 values disclosed.
issues_classified.verification: Section 5.3 discloses that rho=0.924 uses n=6 configs while shuffled control used n=3

issues_classified.description: Polysemanticity stratification AUROC CI is unreliable at n_pos=3. AUROC = 0.922 [0.842, 0.979] on polysemantic latents at L12-16k is presented without noting that n_pos=3 makes bootstrap CIs numerically unreliable. With n_pos < 10, CIs are dominated by sampling artifacts.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a footnote to Section 4.4: 'The polysemantic AUROC CI [0.842, 0.979] derives from n_pos=3 positive examples; bootstrap CIs with n_pos < 10 are unreliable and should be interpreted as illustrative only.' Consider moving the polysemantic AUROC to an appendix, retaining only the monosemantic result (n_pos=13, more defensible) in the main text. Estimated effort: 15 minutes. Verification: Footnote added; reader not misled by CI precision.
issues_classified.verification: Footnote added to Section 4.4 noting n_pos=3 CI unreliability

issues_classified.description: 'Reframes the absorption problem' overclaims from two configurations. Sections 6.3 and Conclusion use language implying a field-level conclusion based on n=16 (non-significant) and n=65 latents in two SAE configurations with a threshold-sensitive finding. This was flagged in iter_0 lessons and remains unchanged.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace 'reframes the absorption problem' with 'provides preliminary evidence that the dominant failure mode is a training-time dictionary coverage gap.' Qualify: 'If the early-type dominance (72-75%) at the canonical tau=0.3 threshold replicates across SAE configurations and architectures, it would redirect attention toward dictionary-coverage solutions. The current finding from two configurations is suggestive rather than definitive.' Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: No 'reframes' language in Sections 6.3 and Conclusion; qualified language used instead.
issues_classified.verification: No 'reframes the problem/field' language; replaced with 'preliminary evidence' qualification

issues_classified.description: GPU parallelization not utilized. All 22 completed tasks ran sequentially on a single GPU (GPU 5). Phase 3 city subtasks (continent/country/language), Phase 5 GPT-2, and R4 subtasks (r4a, r4b, r4c) are mutually independent and could have run in parallel. Estimated wallclock saving: 40-60% if 2+ GPUs available.
issues_classified.suggestion: For iter_2: update task_plan.json to mark Phase 3 subtasks (city_continent, city_country, city_language) as parallel-eligible. Mark Phase 5 GPT-2 replication as parallel-eligible with Phase 3. Mark R4 subtasks (access check, RAVEL probes, Llama replication) as parallel-eligible. Update experiment dispatch to allocate independent tasks to separate GPUs when available. If single GPU remains constraint, accept sequential scheduling but document parallelization potential for future multi-GPU runs. Estimated effort: 1 hour planning update. Verification: task_plan.json contains parallel_group annotations for independent tasks.
issues_classified.verification: task_plan.json contains parallel_group annotations; independent tasks dispatched to separate GPUs when available

issues_classified.description: Blocking corrections from action plan were not enforced before writing phase. iter_0 action plan listed EDA ordering correction (issue_02) as BLOCKING before further writing, but the writing agent proceeded without applying this correction. The factual error persists in the final paper.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a mandatory pre-writing gate: before starting the writing agent, run a machine-readable check against the top-N blocking issues from action_plan.json. If any blocking issue is unresolved (i.e., the condition field does not pass), the pipeline should not advance to writing. Implement as a bash verification step in the orchestrator's writing-entry handler: check that phase2a_taxonomy.json eda_ordering_holds field is consistent with the paper draft's Section 6.2 text. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours (orchestrator script update). Verification: The pipeline refuses to start writing if blocking issues are flagged as unresolved in action_plan.json.
issues_classified.verification: Pre-writing check script implemented; EDA ordering error caught before writing agent starts

issues_classified.description: Intra-RAVEL Bonferroni correction: one of three pairwise Spearman comparisons (city-continent vs. city-language: rho=0.886, p=0.019) does NOT survive Bonferroni correction at 0.0071 threshold. Paper uses 'highly coherent' language without noting this. Only 2/3 pairwise comparisons are Bonferroni-significant.
issues_classified.suggestion: Report each pairwise Bonferroni result explicitly: 'city-country vs. city-language (rho=0.943, p=0.0048) and city-continent vs. city-country (rho=0.943, p=0.0048) survive Bonferroni correction at alpha=0.05/7=0.0071; city-continent vs. city-language (rho=0.886, p=0.019) does not.' Adjust 'highly coherent' to 'coherent for most hierarchy pairs (2/3 pairs survive Bonferroni correction).' Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: Each pairwise result reported with Bonferroni p threshold.
issues_classified.verification: Each pairwise Bonferroni result reported individually; 'highly coherent' qualified

issues_classified.description: Abstract is 247 words vs. NeurIPS 200-word target. ITAC detail in abstract is 3 sentences for a negative result that should be one clause. Code release sentence belongs in Conclusion, not abstract.
issues_classified.suggestion: Trim to 200 words: (1) Replace the 3-sentence ITAC block with: 'An attempted inference-time correction achieves only 3.14% mean FN reduction, revealing that late-type encoder suppression exceeds what linear projection can recover.' (2) Move code release sentence to Conclusion. (3) Verify abstract word count after other fixes (issues 01 and 08 will also reduce length). Estimated effort: 1 hour. Verification: Abstract word count <= 200.
issues_classified.verification: Abstract word count <= 200

issues_fixed: Figures 5, 6, 7 now confirmed as existing PDFs in writing/figures/ — was iter_0 issue_09 blocking item
H3 (cross-domain falsification) properly executed via R4B shuffled hierarchy control (0/9 domain-SAE combinations pass) — was iter_0 issue_01 critical path
GPT-2 Small L6 exact-label validation (AUROC=0.629, n_pos=67) added as open-model anchor — was iter_0 issue_01 mitigation strategy
Paper restructured to honest two-contribution framing (EDA regime-specific + taxonomy); ITAC demoted to Section 7.3 negative results — was iter_0 issue_04
EDA regime-specific framing implemented in abstract and Introduction — was iter_0 issue_03 (partially)
Proxy label caveats added throughout for Gemma Scope results — addresses reproducibility transparency
SynthSAEBench tautology partially acknowledged — was iter_0 issue_13 (partially)
Result debate (optimist/skeptic/strategist/comparativist/revisionist/synthesizer) effectively filtered over-optimistic bias — success pattern retained
success_patterns: Honest negative result reporting in Section 7.3: three pre-registered hypotheses falsified (H3, H5, H6) with specific targets vs. actual results — reviewer feedback confirms this as the paper's strongest section

R4 adaptive remediation round: GPT-2 exact-label validation and shuffled control were not in original plan but were identified and executed correctly — demonstrates system's ability to self-correct experimental gaps

Statistical methodology: bootstrap CIs (10k resamples), DeLong test, Cohen's d, Mann-Whitney U, Kruskal-Wallis, shuffled null column in Table 1 — appropriate and exemplary; shuffled null column is a model of methodological rigor
GPT-2 as open-model anchor strategy: exact-label validation on accessible models provides the most credible evidence and should anchor every future experimental design
Result debate mechanism effective at filtering overconfidence — retain in all future iterations
systemic_patterns: Model access constraints must be resolved or mitigated in the planning stage, not discovered mid-experiment — Gemma gating has been the single largest quality bottleneck across two iterations
Blocking issues from action plans are not being enforced as pre-stage gates — EDA ordering error was BLOCKING in iter_0 and still passed into the writing stage in iter_1

Five-contribution scope is systematically too wide for the evidence base — each iteration should plan for exactly three primary contributions with independent go/no-go criteria and explicit fallback paths
Theorem 1 proof status is the most persistent recurring issue — the decision to retain it as a formal theorem without proof is the primary novelty risk in every review pass

bottleneck_stages: pre-writing correction enforcement (blocking issues not applied)
suggestions: Annotate task_plan.json with parallel_group for independent tasks (Phase 3 subtasks, Phase 5, R4 subtasks)
Add mandatory pre-writing gate checking action_plan.json blocking issues
Schedule r4d_itac_real_activations as required (not optional) in iter_2
Request 2+ GPUs for parallel dispatch when planning multi-phase experiments

recommended_focus: BLOCKING: Correct EDA ordering factual error in Section 6.2 and Table 3 BEFORE any writing in iter_2 (verify against phase2a_taxonomy.json eda_ordering_holds field)
BLOCKING: Fix abstract DeLong comparison (+0.396 → +0.553 at L12-16k) before any submission
BLOCKING: Generate Figure 1 PDF (method diagram for absorption mechanism and EDA geometry)
HIGH: Write Appendix B (Theorem 1 genuine proof or downgrade to Proposition; D-EDA conditioning analysis)
HIGH: Expand taxonomy analysis to all 6 Gemma Scope + GPT-2 L6 configurations (~2-3 GPU hours)
HIGH: Add EDA/SAEBench metric disclosure paragraph to Section 3.1 and revise Introduction
HIGH: Resolve D-EDA/EDA equivalence: verify R3 D-EDA implementation against R4 finding (delta=0.0)
MEDIUM: Add AUPRC as co-primary metric in Table 1 for 65k-width configurations
MEDIUM: Correct Section 7.1 '56%/20x' figures with correct arithmetic from Prec@50 data
MEDIUM: Write Appendix C (RAVEL probe accuracy table + full 9-row shuffled control results)
MEDIUM: Add DeLong citation at first use; verify/resolve SynthSAEBench citation
MEDIUM: Fix 'reframes the problem' language to 'preliminary evidence'
LOW: Implement pre-writing blocking-issue gate in orchestrator pipeline
